title: Painful Swelling - Subcutaneous Abscess
chief_complaint: Painful red swelling on left forearm for 5 days, increasing in size. Now feels soft in the center. Low-grade fever.

physical_exam.skin: 5cm erythematous, warm, tender swelling on forearm with central fluctuance, surrounding induration

hidden_data.us_soft_tissue: Subcutaneous fluid collection 4x3cm with surrounding hyperemia

key_learning_points: Pack larger abscesses, smaller ones heal by secondary intention

patient_responses.onset: Started as small red bump 5 days ago

feedback.incorrect_message: This is an abscess. The fluctuance indicates pus that needs drainage.
feedback.attending_note: Abscess: I&D with local anesthesia. Pack with iodoform gauze if >3cm. Remove packing in 24-48h. Warm compresses. Antibiotics only if cellulitis, immunocompromised, or systemic symptoms.
